items.question: What does early stopping monitor to decide when to halt training?
options: Training loss only
A validation metric such as val/loss or val/f1 each evaluation pass
Test set accuracy every epoch
Number of training rows
items.answer: A validation metric such as val/loss or val/f1 each evaluation pass
items.explanation: Early stopping uses validation performance — not training loss or test metrics — as the overfitting signal.

items.question: What is patience in early stopping?
options: The maximum number of epochs to train regardless of validation
The number of consecutive evaluation passes without beating the best validation score before stopping
The learning rate decay schedule
The size of the validation set
items.answer: The number of consecutive evaluation passes without beating the best validation score before stopping
items.explanation: Patience counts how long to wait after validation stops improving before halting training.

items.question: Why restore best weights after early stopping?
options: Training loss is lowest at the final epoch
The final epoch often has worse validation than an earlier best epoch
Test set requires the last epoch for fairness
Restore is only needed for regression tasks
items.answer: The final epoch often has worse validation than an earlier best epoch
items.explanation: Training continues for patience passes after the best validation epoch — final weights are typically overfit.

items.question: Which Keras callback setting reloads the best validation checkpoint?
options: EarlyStopping(monitor="loss")
EarlyStopping(restore_best_weights=True)
ReduceLROnPlateau(patience=0)
ModelCheckpoint(save_best_only=False)
items.explanation: restore_best_weights=True reloads weights from the epoch with the best monitored validation metric.

items.question: In XGBoost, which attribute gives the round count for prediction after early stopping?
options: n_estimators
best_iteration
learning_rate
max_depth
items.explanation: best_iteration marks the round with best validation performance — use it to exclude late overfitting trees.

items.question: Why should you not use the test set for early stopping?
options: Test labels are unavailable during training
Repeated test evaluation leaks information into model selection like test-set tuning
Test rows are too small for any metric
Frameworks forbid test data in memory
items.answer: Repeated test evaluation leaks information into model selection like test-set tuning
items.explanation: The test set must stay locked — validation absorbs iterative feedback during training.
